---
title: "original wide to long conversion script"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

# ── 1. Load 2024 sheets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
path_2024 <- here("rawdata", "2024sheets")
files_2024 <- list.files(path_2024, pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE)

raw_list <- map(files_2024, ~ {
  meta <- read_excel(.x, n_max = 1, col_names = FALSE, col_types = "text")
  obs_pos <- which(meta[1, ] == "Obs:")
  obs_val <- if (length(obs_pos) > 0 && obs_pos + 1 <= ncol(meta)) {
    as.character(meta[1, obs_pos + 1])
  } else {
    NA_character_
  }
  read_excel(.x, skip = 1, col_types = "text") |>
    mutate(obs = obs_val, source_file = basename(.x))
})
names(raw_list) <- basename(files_2024)

# ── 2. Diagnose ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
reference_cols <- names(raw_list[[1]])

map(names(raw_list), ~ {
  diff <- setdiff(names(raw_list[[.x]]), reference_cols)
  miss <- setdiff(reference_cols, names(raw_list[[.x]]))
  if (length(diff) > 0) cat(.x, "| extra:", diff, "\n")
  if (length(miss) > 0) cat(.x, "| missing:", miss, "\n")
})

# ── 3. Standardize column names ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Coalesce measurement variants (L2024, L, L24 all mean the same thing)
raw_2024 <- bind_rows(raw_list) |>
  rename(
    site     = Site,
    habitat  = Hab,
    transect = Tran,
    taxa     = Taxa,
    x        = X,
    y        = Y,
    z        = Z,
    status   = Status,
    note     = Notes
  ) |>
  mutate(
    L        = coalesce(L2024, L, L24),
    W        = coalesce(W2024, W, W24),
    H        = coalesce(H2024, H, H24),
    dynamics = coalesce(Dynam, Dynamic, Dyn, Dynamics),
    year     = 2024L,
    across(c(x, y, z, L, W, H), as.numeric)
  ) |>
  select(-any_of(c("L2024", "W2024", "H2024", "L24", "W24", "H24",
                   "Dynam", "Dynamic", "Dyn", "Dynamics", "...17",
                   "status", "source_file")))

# ── 4. Inspect ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
glimpse(raw_2024)
unique(raw_2024$site)
unique(raw_2024$habitat)
unique(raw_2024$transect)
unique(raw_2024$taxa)
```
